title: Setting Up 2FA
content:
Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) is one of the most effective ways to protect an account from unauthorized access. Even if a password is stolen or guessed, 2FA prevents login unless the attacker also has access to the user's authenticator device. Tklesson supports TOTP-based 2FA, which works with any standard authenticator app.

Recommended Authenticator Apps

  • Google Authenticator — Free, simple, available on iOS and Android.
  • Authy — Supports encrypted cloud backups and multi-device sync.
  • Microsoft Authenticator — Integrates well with Microsoft accounts and offers push notifications.
  • Duo Mobile — Enterprise-grade with backup and recovery options.

Step-by-Step Setup

  1. Log in to your Tklesson dashboard.
  2. Navigate to Settings or Account Security.
  3. Under the Two-Factor Authentication section, click Enable.
  4. A QR code will appear on screen. Open your authenticator app and select Add Account or the + icon.
  5. Scan the QR code with your phone's camera. If scanning is not possible, you can manually enter the secret key displayed below the QR code.
  6. The app will generate a 6-digit code that refreshes every 30 seconds. Enter this code on the Tklesson setup screen and click Verify.
  7. Once verified, a set of recovery codes will be displayed. These are single-use codes that allow you to log in if you lose access to your authenticator app.

Saving Recovery Codes

Recovery codes are the only way to regain access if your authenticator device is lost or reset. Treat them with the same care as your password:

  • Download or copy the codes and store them in a secure location.
  • Do not store them on the same device you use for authentication — if the device is lost, the codes are lost too.
  • Recommended storage options: A password manager, an encrypted USB drive, a printed copy kept in a safe location, or a secure cloud storage service.
  • Each recovery code can only be used once. After use, generate a new set from your security settings.

Important Reminders

  • Tklesson staff will never ask for your 2FA codes or recovery codes. If someone requests them, it is a scam.
  • If you change phones, disable 2FA first from your old device, then re-enable it on the new one — or use an authenticator app that supports cloud backup like Authy.
  • If you lose both your device and your recovery codes, contact support. An agent will verify your identity and disable 2FA so you can re-enroll.
